= Redis =

Redis is a high-performance, in-memory data store that DreamFactory uses for caching, session storage, and rate limiting. It provides sub-millisecond response times and supports advanced data structures for complex caching scenarios.

----

== Use Cases ==

* '''API response caching''': Store database query results for faster subsequent requests
* '''Session storage''': Distribute user sessions across multiple DreamFactory instances
* '''Rate limiting''': Track API request counts with automatic expiry
* '''Queue backend''': Process background jobs asynchronously
* '''Pub/Sub messaging''': Real-time event distribution (advanced)

----

== Prerequisites ==

=== Redis Server ===

Install Redis on your server or use a managed service:

'''Ubuntu/Debian:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
sudo apt update
sudo apt install redis-server
sudo systemctl enable redis-server
sudo systemctl start redis-server
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Managed Services:'''
* AWS ElastiCache for Redis
* Azure Cache for Redis
* Google Cloud Memorystore
* Redis Cloud

=== Verify Installation ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
redis-cli ping
# Expected response: PONG
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Configuring Redis in DreamFactory ==

=== System Cache Configuration ===

Set Redis as the system cache backend in your DreamFactory environment file (<code>.env</code>):

<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
CACHE_DRIVER=redis
REDIS_HOST=localhost
REDIS_PORT=6379
REDIS_PASSWORD=your_secure_password
REDIS_DATABASE=0
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Configuration Options ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Variable !! Required !! Default !! Description
|-
| <code>CACHE_DRIVER</code> || Yes || <code>file</code> || Set to <code>redis</code> to enable
|-
| <code>REDIS_HOST</code> || Yes || <code>localhost</code> || Redis server hostname or IP
|-
| <code>REDIS_PORT</code> || No || <code>6379</code> || Redis server port
|-
| <code>REDIS_PASSWORD</code> || No || - || Redis authentication password
|-
| <code>REDIS_DATABASE</code> || No || <code>0</code> || Redis database number (0-15)
|-
| <code>REDIS_PREFIX</code> || No || <code>dreamfactory</code> || Key prefix for namespacing
|}

=== Session Storage ===

To store sessions in Redis:

<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
SESSION_DRIVER=redis
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Queue Backend ===

To use Redis for background job queues:

<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
QUEUE_DRIVER=redis
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Creating a Redis Cache Service ==

In addition to system caching, you can create a Redis service for direct cache API access.

=== Step 1: Navigate to API Generation ===

Log in to your DreamFactory instance and select '''API Generation & Connections'''. Set API Type to '''Cache'''.

=== Step 2: Create New Service ===

Click the plus button and select '''Redis'''.

=== Step 3: Configure Service Details ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Field !! Description !! Example
|-
| Name || Service name (used in API URL) || <code>redis</code>
|-
| Label || Display name || <code>Redis Cache</code>
|-
| Description || Service description || <code>Application cache service</code>
|}

=== Step 4: Configure Connection ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Field !! Required !! Default !! Description
|-
| Host || No || <code>127.0.0.1</code> || Redis server hostname or IP
|-
| Port || No || <code>6379</code> || Redis port number
|-
| Password || No || - || Authentication password
|-
| Database Index || No || <code>0</code> || Redis database number (0-15)
|-
| Default TTL || No || <code>300</code> || Time to live in minutes before cached values expire
|}

=== Step 5: Save and Test ===

Save the service and use API Docs to test cache operations.

----

== API Endpoints ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Method !! Endpoint !! Description
|-
| <code>GET</code> || <code>/api/v2/{service}/</code> || List all keys (use sparingly)
|-
| <code>GET</code> || <code>/api/v2/{service}/{key}</code> || Get value by key
|-
| <code>POST</code> || <code>/api/v2/{service}</code> || Store one or more key-value pairs
|-
| <code>PUT</code> || <code>/api/v2/{service}/{key}</code> || Update existing key
|-
| <code>DELETE</code> || <code>/api/v2/{service}/{key}</code> || Delete a key
|-
| <code>DELETE</code> || <code>/api/v2/{service}</code> || Flush all keys (dangerous)
|}

----

== API Examples ==

=== Store a Value ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X POST "https://example.com/api/v2/redis" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "resource": [
      {
        "key": "user:123:profile",
        "value": {"name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com"},
        "ttl": 3600
      }
    ]
  }'
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "key": "user:123:profile",
      "success": true
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Store Multiple Values ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X POST "https://example.com/api/v2/redis" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "resource": [
      {"key": "setting:theme", "value": "dark", "ttl": 86400},
      {"key": "setting:language", "value": "en", "ttl": 86400},
      {"key": "setting:timezone", "value": "UTC", "ttl": 86400}
    ]
  }'
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Retrieve a Value ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/redis/user:123:profile" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY"
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "key": "user:123:profile",
  "value": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com"
  }
}
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Delete a Key ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X DELETE "https://example.com/api/v2/redis/user:123:profile" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY"
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Check if Key Exists ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/redis/user:123:profile?check_exist=true" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY"
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "key": "user:123:profile",
  "exists": true
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== TTL (Time-To-Live) ==

Set expiration times when storing values:

{| class="wikitable"
! TTL Value !! Behavior
|-
| Positive integer || Expire after N seconds
|-
| <code>0</code> || No expiration (persist indefinitely)
|-
| <code>-1</code> || Use default TTL from service config
|}

=== Recommended TTL Values ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Data Type !! TTL (seconds) !! Duration
|-
| Session data || 1800 || 30 minutes
|-
| User profiles || 300 || 5 minutes
|-
| Reference data || 86400 || 24 hours
|-
| Rate limit counters || 60 || 1 minute
|-
| Real-time data || 30 || 30 seconds
|}

----

== Redis Configuration Best Practices ==

=== Security ===

'''Bind to localhost or private network:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
# /etc/redis/redis.conf
bind 127.0.0.1
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Require authentication:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
requirepass your_strong_password_here
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Disable dangerous commands:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
rename-command FLUSHDB ""
rename-command FLUSHALL ""
rename-command DEBUG ""
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Memory Management ===

'''Set memory limit:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
maxmemory 256mb
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Configure eviction policy:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
</syntaxhighlight>

{| class="wikitable"
! Policy !! Description
|-
| <code>noeviction</code> || Return error when memory limit reached
|-
| <code>allkeys-lru</code> || Evict least recently used keys (recommended)
|-
| <code>volatile-lru</code> || Evict LRU keys with TTL set
|-
| <code>allkeys-random</code> || Random eviction
|}

=== Persistence ===

'''RDB snapshots (default):'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
save 900 1    # Save if 1 key changed in 900 seconds
save 300 10   # Save if 10 keys changed in 300 seconds
save 60 10000 # Save if 10000 keys changed in 60 seconds
</syntaxhighlight>

'''AOF for durability:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
appendonly yes
appendfsync everysec
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== High Availability ==

=== Redis Sentinel ===

For automatic failover:

<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
REDIS_SENTINEL_HOST=sentinel1.example.com,sentinel2.example.com
REDIS_SENTINEL_PORT=26379
REDIS_SENTINEL_MASTER=mymaster
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Redis Cluster ===

For horizontal scaling, configure multiple Redis nodes with clustering enabled.

----

== Monitoring ==

=== Key Metrics to Watch ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Metric !! Description !! Alert Threshold
|-
| <code>used_memory</code> || Current memory usage || > 80% of maxmemory
|-
| <code>connected_clients</code> || Active connections || > 80% of maxclients
|-
| <code>keyspace_hits</code> || Cache hit count || -
|-
| <code>keyspace_misses</code> || Cache miss count || High miss ratio
|-
| <code>evicted_keys</code> || Keys removed due to memory || Any eviction
|}

=== Redis CLI Commands ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
# Check server status
redis-cli info

# Monitor memory
redis-cli info memory

# Check connected clients
redis-cli client list

# Get hit/miss ratio
redis-cli info stats | grep keyspace
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Common Errors ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Error Code !! Message !! Cause !! Solution
|-
| 400 || Bad Request || Invalid key or value || Check request format
|-
| 401 || Unauthorized || Missing API key || Add API key header
|-
| 404 || Not Found || Key does not exist || Handle cache miss
|-
| 500 || Connection refused || Redis server unreachable || Check Redis server status
|-
| 500 || NOAUTH || Authentication required || Add REDIS_PASSWORD to .env
|}

=== Troubleshooting Connection Issues ===

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
# Test Redis connectivity
redis-cli -h localhost -p 6379 ping

# Test with password
redis-cli -h localhost -p 6379 -a your_password ping

# Check Redis logs
sudo tail -f /var/log/redis/redis-server.log
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Performance Tuning ==

=== Connection Pooling ===

Configure persistent connections in Laravel/DreamFactory:

<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
REDIS_CLIENT=phpredis
</syntaxhighlight>

=== Pipelining ===

For bulk operations, the API supports batch requests that are automatically pipelined.

=== Key Design ===

* Use colons <code>:</code> as separators: <code>user:123:profile</code>
* Keep keys short but descriptive
* Use consistent naming conventions
* Avoid spaces and special characters

----

== Next Steps ==

* '''[[Cache Overview]]''': Compare caching backends
* '''[[Memcached]]''': Alternative caching option
* '''[[Getting_Started/Optimizing_Dreamfactory/Database|Performance Tuning]]''': Optimize DreamFactory
